title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hyperthyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hyperthyroidism are concentrated plant extracts used to manage the symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland. They help by regulating thyroid hormone production, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being. The best herbal tinctures for hyperthyroidism include Astragalus membranaceus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Schisandra chinensis, Zingiber officinale, Withania somnifera, Cassia auriculata, Silybum marianum, Cinchona officinalis, Panax ginseng, and Rehmannia glutinosa. If left untreated, hyperthyroidism can cause weight loss, anxiety, tremors, and fatigue, severely impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a natural ability to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany this condition. The herb's adaptogenic properties help regulate cortisol levels, which can become imbalanced in cases of hyperthyroidism. Additionally, ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive thyroid hormone production. By supporting adrenal function and promoting balance, ashwagandha tinctures may help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heart rate, weight loss, and insomnia associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic activity: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help regulate the body's response to stress, which is often exacerbated in hyperthyroidism, reducing symptoms such as anxiety and fatigue.

Thyroid hormone regulation: Ashwagandha has been shown to reduce thyroid hormone levels by inhibiting the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), potentially helping to alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and other parts of the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism-related symptoms, such as joint pain and swelling.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroid glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism and alleviate symptoms like anxiety and tremors.

Alkaloids (Withanine and Withaferin A): These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, thereby reducing their levels in the body and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism like weight loss, heat intolerance, and palpitations.

Saponins (Withanoside E): These glycosides have been found to possess anti-thyroid activity, which may help reduce the production of thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, as well as possess antioxidant properties to reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are primarily used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including withanolides, which help in regulating thyroid function.

Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera are used because they contain significant amounts of withanolides and other polyphenols, which help to mitigate hyperthyroid symptoms such as anxiety and insomnia.

Fruits: The fruits of Withania somnifera are used due to their high antioxidant content, including withanolides and other polyphenols, which can help to balance thyroid hormones and reduce oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried withania somnifera root and 75 percent ethanol as ingredients.
Crush the dried withania somnifera root into a fine powder to increase the surface area.
Combine the powdered root with 500 milliliters of 75 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing its overactivity. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, help to normalize thyroid hormone production, alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss. Additionally, motherwort tinctures may also help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy balance between thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3). This natural approach can provide relief from hyperthyroidism without resorting to pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Motherwort's cardiac glycosides help regulate heart rhythm and reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism-related atrial fibrillation, such as palpitations and arrhythmias.

Flavonoids and saponins: The flavonoids and saponins present in motherwort tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms like heat intolerance, anxiety, and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism.

Phenolic acids: Motherwort's phenolic acids, particularly rosmarinic acid, exhibit thyroid-suppressive activity, which can help reduce the production of thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and tremors.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that helps mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in the production of thyroid hormones, thus potentially reducing hyperthyroid symptoms.
Rosavin: A phenolic glycoside that has been reported to have a positive effect on the thyroid gland by reducing thyroid hormone production and improving thyroid function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to regulate thyroid function.
Roots: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their rich content of iridoid glycosides, which have a balancing effect on thyroid hormones.

Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to reduce thyroid-stimulating hormone and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried leonurus cardiaca herb and place it in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Fill a separate glass container with 40% ethanol and 60% distilled water to create a solvent mixture.
Combine the dried leonurus cardiaca herb with the solvent mixture in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because of its ability to balance thyroid function. The essential oils present in thyme, particularly thymol, have been shown to inhibit the production of excessive thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, thyme's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate thyroid dysfunction. By promoting healthy thyroid function, herbal thyme tinctures offer a natural and potentially effective way to manage hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms like fatigue, weight loss, and anxiety.

Adaptogenic properties: Thyme's adaptogenic effects can help the body adapt to stress and balance cortisol levels, which may alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, tremors, and mood changes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which may alleviate symptoms like swelling and pain in the neck.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a phenolic monoterpene, helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol, a phenolic monoterpene, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms such as thyroid gland inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound, has been shown to have a potential anti-thyroid effect by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, which can help regulate thyroid hormone levels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of thymol, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms.
Buds: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of thymol and other essential oils compared to the leaves, making them more potent for treating hyperthyroidism.
Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of thymol and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help mitigate hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency when in bloom.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces to release their essential oils and increase extraction efficiency.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 cups of vodka 80 proof and 1 cup of glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing symptoms of anxiety and restlessness commonly associated with this condition. The tannins in lemon balm also help to regulate thyroid hormone production by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in thyroxine synthesis. Additionally, lemon balm's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress caused by increased metabolic activity in hyperthyroidism, promoting a sense of balance and calmness in the body.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in managing the emotional symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

Adrenal Support and Stress Reduction: Lemon balm has been shown to support adrenal function and reduce stress levels, which can help alleviate the physical and emotional symptoms of hyperthyroidism caused by an overactive thyroid gland.

Hormone Regulation and Balancing Properties: Lemon balm has been traditionally used to regulate hormonal imbalances, including thyroid hormones, and may help normalize thyroid function in individuals with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with hyperthyroidism by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, thus alleviating symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid.

Luteolin: This flavonoid constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help regulate thyroid function and reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and rapid heartbeat.

Melissic acid: Although less researched than other constituents, melissic acid has shown potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which could contribute to its potential benefits in managing hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Roots: The roots are also used, as they contain a variety of compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help regulate thyroid function.
Buds: The buds are another part used, as they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the leaves in a single layer in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried melissa leaves with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it targets the root cause of the issue, which is often an imbalance in thyroid hormones. The tincture's natural diuretic properties help to flush out excess fluid and reduce swelling caused by hypothyroidism. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties soothe inflammation and calm down the immune system, promoting a more balanced thyroid function. By supporting healthy digestion and liver detoxification, dandelion tinctures also aid in the body's natural elimination of toxins that may be contributing to hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture helps to reduce thyroid swelling and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and water retention by increasing urine production, which can help to reduce pressure on the thyroid gland.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tincture help to protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress that exacerbates hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Choleretic properties: Dandelion tincture stimulates the production of bile, which helps to improve liver function and reduce the burden on the thyroid gland, allowing it to function more efficiently and reducing symptoms such as heat intolerance and anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in dandelion, such as quercetin, may help alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with thyroid disorders.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including chlorogenic acid, in dandelion may aid in managing hyperthyroidism by modulating thyroid hormone production and reducing oxidative damage to thyroid tissues.
Saponins: Saponins present in dandelion may help alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms by reducing thyroid hormone production and absorption, as well as exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have a modulating effect on thyroid function.
Roots: The roots contain inulin and other compounds that help to reduce thyroid activity and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Flowers: The flowers have been used to create tinctures due to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to regulate thyroid hormone production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in late spring or early summer when flowers are in full bloom.
Chop 100 grams of the plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has been traditionally used to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. The active compound, guggulsterone, is believed to work by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, thereby decreasing the levels of free thyroxine in the blood. Additionally, guggul tinctures may help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms of hyperthyroidism. By reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating symptoms, guggul tinctures can provide relief from the uncomfortable and debilitating effects of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Guggul's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as guggulusterones and gum guggul, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant properties: Guggul's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and thyroid damage, helping to prevent or reverse thyroid dysfunction.

Adaptogenic properties: Guggul's adaptogenic compounds, such as beta-amyrin, can help regulate the body's response to stress, reducing the impact of hyperthyroidism on the adrenal glands and promoting overall thyroid health.
remedy_constituents: Guggulsterones: Guggulsterones in Commiphora mukul tinctures are believed to help with hyperthyroidism by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, which is an overactive form of thyroid hormone that can cause hyperthyroid symptoms.
Commiphoric acid: Commiphoric acid may help alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland.

Guggul resin's sesquiterpenes: The sesquiterpenes present in guggul resin, a key component of Commiphora mukul, may help mitigate hyperthyroid symptoms by modulating the thyroid hormone axis and reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH).
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Commiphora mukul are commonly used to make tinctures, as they contain guggulsterones, which help regulate thyroid function and reduce inflammation.

Buds: The buds of the plant are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to manage hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried commiphora mukul root bark in the early morning for optimal potency.
Chop the dried root bark into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped commiphora mukul root bark with 500 ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, which can help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heart rate, weight loss, and anxiety. Additionally, licorice tinctures may also help improve adrenal function, promoting a sense of balance and calmness in individuals with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hyperthyroidism.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licochalcone A, which have been shown to inhibit the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), thereby reducing the production of excess thyroid hormones.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid found in licorice root, have been found to decrease the activity of enzymes involved in the synthesis of cortisol and other hormones, which can help reduce the symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, insomnia, and weight loss.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that can contribute to an overactive thyroid gland.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly isoflavones, in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help regulate thyroid hormone production and reduce oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, may help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms by inhibiting the activity of 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme involved in the regulation of cortisol and thyroid hormone production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to block the conversion of T4 to T3, thereby reducing thyroid hormone levels.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used to enhance the effects of glycyrrhizin, allowing for a more effective treatment of hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to balance the hormonal effects of glycyrrhizin, preventing its side effects while maintaining its therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and chop them into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine 100 grams of chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Filter the tincture through a 0.2-micron filter and store it in a dark glass bottle away from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), thereby reducing the excessive production of thyroid hormones. Additionally, maypop's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy balance of thyroid hormone levels and alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety often associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Maypop's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction in people with hyperthyroidism.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The calming compounds in Maypop tincture, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, can help alleviate anxiety and restlessness, common symptoms of hyperthyroidism, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress on the thyroid gland.

Adrenal support: Maypop's ability to support adrenal function, particularly by reducing cortisol levels, can help mitigate the effects of excessive thyroid hormone production in hyperthyroidism, promoting overall hormonal balance.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

Alkaloids (specifically, harmine and harmaline): These alkaloids have a sedative effect on the nervous system and may help reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate hyperthyroidism symptoms. They also exhibit anti-adrenergic properties, which may help mitigate the sympathetic nervous system's overactivity associated with hyperthyroidism.

Terpenoids (specifically, passiflorine and passicol): These terpenoids have a calming effect on the nervous system and may help reduce anxiety and stress. They also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other active compounds that help in calming the nervous system and reducing thyroid hormone production.
Stems: Stems contain bioactive compounds that may help in reducing the symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and insomnia, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Barks: Barks are used for their sedative properties, which may help in calming the nervous system and reducing thyroid hormone production, thereby alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Dry the passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the passiflora incarnata tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because they have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia often associated with an overactive thyroid gland. Additionally, valerian has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate joint pain and stiffness commonly experienced by individuals with hyperthyroidism. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Valerian helps to balance the body's response to stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hyperthyroid symptoms like insomnia and restlessness.

Anxiolytic properties: The sedative compounds in valerian tincture, such as valepotriates and isovaleric acid, help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and irritability common in hyperthyroid patients.
Antioxidant activity: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by thyroid hormone imbalances, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, helps alleviate anxiety and stress associated with hyperthyroidism by modulating the GABA receptor and reducing cortisol levels.

Valeranone: Valeranone, a valerenic acid derivative, has shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help mitigate the anxiety and restlessness symptoms often experienced by individuals with hyperthyroidism.

Isovalerenic acid: Isovalerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, potentially helping to reduce the insomnia and restlessness associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of valerenic acid, which is believed to have a sedative effect and can help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Stems: The stems also contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds, making them a secondary choice for tinctures due to their potential to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves may be used as a less potent alternative to the roots and stems, as they still contain valerenic acid and other compounds that can contribute to a calming effect on the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 roots of valeriana officinalis in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 100g of chopped roots with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking it daily for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the activity of thyroid hormones. This natural herb has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to calm an overactive thyroid gland, reducing symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss. The tincture's antioxidant properties also help to mitigate inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy balance between production and regulation of thyroid hormones. By addressing the root cause of hyperthyroidism, maidenhair tree tinctures offer a natural and holistic approach to managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect cells from oxidative damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone production, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair Tree's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, heat intolerance, and muscle weakness associated with hyperthyroidism.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism by neutralizing free radicals and improving antioxidant defenses in the body.

Bilobalide: This terpene may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which could contribute to the development of hyperthyroidism, while also improving blood flow and reducing symptoms like anxiety and tremors.

Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin may help stabilize thyroid hormones and regulate the immune system's response, which could aid in managing hyperthyroidism symptoms like weight loss, anxiety, and rapid heart rate.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 500 ml of 80 proof vodka for tincture base.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into fine pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because of its anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help to reduce the symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. The tincture's flavonoids and terpenoids have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, thereby reducing hyperthyroidism's characteristic symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and insomnia. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can also help to alleviate stress and promote a sense of relaxation, further aiding in the management of hyperthyroidism-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin, can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: Chamomile's calming and relaxing properties can help alleviate anxiety and stress, common symptoms of hyperthyroidism, allowing individuals to better cope with their condition.

Antioxidant activity: Chamomile's antioxidants can help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and thyroid dysfunction in cases of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and anxiety associated with hyperthyroidism.
Roots: Contain a significant amount of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help regulate gut health and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and measure out 100 grams of material for the extraction process.
Combine the measured flowers with 700 milliliters of 60-70% ethanol in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, label and store in a cool dark place, ready for use at a 1:3 dilution ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has natural antithyroid properties that can help regulate thyroid function. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), which is overproduced in individuals with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and heat intolerance associated with this condition. By supporting the body's natural thyroid function, St John's Wort tinctures may help reduce thyrotoxicosis and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort has antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction.

Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort is known for its adaptogenic effects, helping the body adapt to stress and promoting balance in hormonal regulation, including thyroid hormones. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals with hyperthyroidism who often experience stress-related symptoms.

Thyroid hormone regulating properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to modulate thyroid hormone production and metabolism, potentially reducing excessive thyroxine (T4) levels and alleviating symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Flowers: The flowers of St John's Wort are rich in hyperforin and adhyperforin, compounds that have been found to inhibit the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), helping to regulate thyroid hormone levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality st john's wort flowers at peak blooming time for maximum potency.
Chop the fresh or dried flowers into small pieces to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine one part chopped herb with three parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily, to allow for steeping.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle and discard solids.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Kava's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can be exacerbated by hyperthyroidism and contribute to symptoms like fatigue and muscle weakness.

Adaptogenic Properties: Kava's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stress and anxiety related to hyperthyroidism, promoting balance and reducing cortisol levels, which can become imbalanced due to thyroid dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and sedative effect, which can help alleviate anxiety and restlessness associated with hyperthyroidism.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the kava plant that contain high concentrations of active compounds like kavalactones, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress in individuals with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper methysticum leaves from a trusted source or purchase high-quality dried leaves.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped piper methysticum leaves with 2 parts 190-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks to allow for thorough maceration and tincture formation.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container discarding the solids and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has been traditionally used to support adrenal function, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The adaptogenic properties of Siberian ginseng help the body cope with stress and normalize cortisol levels, reducing the negative impact of excess thyroid hormones on the body. Additionally, Siberian ginseng tinctures may also help regulate metabolism and energy production, providing a natural and gentle approach to managing hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Siberian ginseng tincture helps regulate the body's response to stress, reducing the negative impact of hyperthyroidism on the nervous system.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help mitigate thyroid hormone-induced oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate hyperthyroid symptoms.

Cardiovascular protection: Siberian ginseng has been shown to improve cardiovascular function by reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and protecting against lipid peroxidation, all of which are beneficial for individuals with hyperthyroidism who may experience cardiac issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are believed to help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Rhizomes: The rhizome's unique chemical composition is thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe the body's response to hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean fresh eleutherococcus senticosus roots and stems in the morning for maximum potency.
Chop the cleaned herb into small pieces and combine 50 grams with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or everclear.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking daily, at room temperature.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper cap and label, keeping refrigerated for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate thyroid issues. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to balance the body's response to stress, which is often elevated in individuals with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Brahmi has been shown to regulate cortisol levels, promoting a sense of calm and stability that can help alleviate symptoms such as insomnia and anxiety attacks. By addressing these underlying issues, Brahmi tinctures may help reduce overall thyroid hormone production and promote a healthier balance in the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals generated by excessive thyroid hormone production, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Hyperthyroidism.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Brahmi's anxiolytic properties may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and irritability commonly experienced in Hyperthyroidism patients, promoting relaxation and calmness.

Adaptogenic and thyroid-regulating actions: Brahmi has been traditionally used to balance the body's energy (prana) and adapt to stressors, which may help regulate thyroid function, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of Brahmi have been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and regulate thyroid hormone production.
Rhyzomes: As a rich source of saponins and flavonoids, rhyzomes of Brahmi help to modulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop 20 grams of bacopa monnieri into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Add 200 milliliters of vodka as a solvent to the jar and stir well to coat all plant material.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days while shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle leaving behind solid residue.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing its excessive activity. The herb's adaptogenic properties help to normalize thyroid function, alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting overall health and well-being. By regulating thyroid function, Asiatic pennywort tinctures may help to alleviate hyperthyroidism-related symptoms and promote a sense of balance and harmony in the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can mitigate thyroid inflammation and damage caused by hyperthyroidism.

Adaptogenic properties: The plant's adaptogenic compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help regulate the body's response to stress, including hormonal imbalances, which can alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism like anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Asiatic Pennywort's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism, and also alleviate associated symptoms like joint pain and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Asiatic pennywort are used in herbal medicine due to their ability to calm hyperthyroid symptoms and regulate thyroid function, making them an effective treatment for hyperthyroidism.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism, making them a valuable component in medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality centella asiatica leaves and stems from a trusted source or cultivate your own.
Cleanly wash the plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt debris or contaminants thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces using scissors or sharp knife to increase surface area significantly.
Combine 1 part chopped centella asiatica with 2 parts vodka in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks shaking daily gently.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the plant material and store the tincture in dark bottle immediately.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperthyroidism that are treated with herbal tinctures are adrenal fatigue, hypothyroidism, and Graves' disease. Herbal remedies such as ashwagandha, ginseng, and licorice root have been traditionally used to support the adrenal glands and balance thyroid function. Other herbs like Bladderwrack, Kombu, and Dulse may help regulate thyroid hormones, while Schisandra and Rhodiola may reduce symptoms of fatigue and anxiety associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with hyperthyroidism is a blend of ashwagandha, bladderwrack, and dandelion root. Ashwagandha helps to reduce cortisol levels and alleviate stress, while bladderwrack supports thyroid function and balances iodine levels. Dandelion root aids in detoxification and promotes a healthy metabolism. Additionally, incorporating licorice root and milk thistle tinctures may also be beneficial, as they help to support adrenal function and liver health, respectively, in conjunction with a balanced diet and a healthcare professional's guidance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it has been traditionally used to support the immune system and adaptogenic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. The tincture is believed to help regulate the body's stress response, potentially reducing the thyroid's production of hormones. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus may help increase the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) in the pituitary gland, which can help balance thyroid function. This may aid in reducing symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Saponins: They have a modulating effect on the immune system, which can help in reducing the production of thyroid hormones and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Polysaccharides: They have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties, which can help in reducing inflammation in the thyroid gland and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are considered the most valuable part of Astragalus membranaceus, as they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid function.
Stems: The stems of Astragalus membranaceus contain isoflavones and flavonoids, which help to balance thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Leaves: The leaves of Astragalus membranaceus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce thyroid inflammation and promote overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:5 or 1:10 ratio of dried astragalus membranaceus roots to solvent mixture by hand or with a tool.
Chop 20 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus roots into fine pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine 20 grams of chopped roots with 100 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and stirring every few days.
Strain the liquid from the plant material and discard the solids by pouring through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains 18-alpha-glycyrrhetinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce thyroid inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss. The tincture's licorice root extracts may also help block the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that can exacerbate hyperthyroid symptoms. Additionally, glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help stabilize thyroid hormone levels, promoting a return to normal thyroid function and reducing hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: Help with hyperthyroidism by modulating the activity of enzymes involved in cortisol and thyroid hormone metabolism, thereby reducing the symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Phenolic acids: Aid in reducing hyperthyroid symptoms by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that contributes to the development of hyperthyroidism.
Flavonoids: Assist in alleviating hyperthyroid symptoms by possessing antioxidant properties that help counteract oxidative stress, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because they are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to block the conversion of thyroid hormones into their active forms.

Barks: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because they contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because they contain glycosides and saponins that have a balancing effect on the thyroid gland and help to regulate its function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 percent of the glycyrrhiza glabra root system in the early morning for optimal quality.
Clean and chop the harvested root into small pieces to release its medicinal properties.
Combine one part of chopped root with one part of vodka in a clean glass container.
Seal the container and store it in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains adaptogenic compounds that support the body's natural response to stress and hormonal imbalances. The tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis is believed to promote the health of the adrenal glands, which can become exhausted in cases of hyperthyroidism, leading to an overproduction of thyroid hormones. By supporting the body's natural balance, Schisandra chinensis tincture may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans have been shown to have a regulatory effect on thyroid hormones, helping to normalize thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Schisandrin B: This lignan has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
Schisandrin A: This lignan has been reported to have a stabilizing effect on the thyroid gland, reducing the production of thyroid hormones and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly schisandrins, which have adaptogenic properties that help balance thyroid function.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain schisandrins and other lignans with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to have a regulating effect on thyroid hormone production and metabolism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried schisandra chinensis berries and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Combine the clean berries with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a dark glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, retaining the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to a clean dark glass bottle and label it for storage and future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, may help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Additionally, the tinctures may help to regulate thyroid function by stimulating the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), thus promoting a balance between thyroid hormones. This natural approach may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as weight loss and anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism, which can contribute to symptoms such as anxiety and weight loss.
Shogaols: Act as a natural adrenergic receptor blocker, potentially helping to mitigate the effects of excessive thyroid hormone on the body, such as rapid heartbeat and tremors.
Volatile oils (specifically, borneol and zingiberene): Exhibit sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as insomnia and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain essential oils and compounds that help in reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating hyperthyroid symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the plant contain a higher concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antithyroid effects, helping to manage hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 root-to-liquor ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle to separate the liquid from solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain adaptogenic compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a balanced metabolic rate. Additionally, Withania somnifera's ability to reduce cortisol levels can help mitigate the negative effects of hyperthyroidism on the adrenal glands, promoting a healthier stress response and overall thyroid function. This may lead to a reduction in symptoms such as anxiety, tremors, and weight loss.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: They help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms by reducing thyroid hormone production and regulating the immune system's response to the thyroid gland.
Withaferins: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
Withanone: It has a modulating effect on the thyroid axis, reducing the secretion of thyroid hormones and thus helping to control the symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate thyroid function.
Leaves: They are used for their adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce stress and promote balance in the body, which can be beneficial in managing hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their high content of withanone, a compound that may help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of withania somnifera roots at the end of the growing season when plants are mature.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 days.
Combine 1 part dried root pieces with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for a ratio of 1:2.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks with occasional shaking every 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to regulate thyroid hormone production and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms. Additionally, Cassia auriculata has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to balance the body's metabolic processes and promote overall well-being. By reducing thyroid hormone levels and promoting a healthy metabolic balance, Cassia auriculata tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as weight loss, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce thyroid inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
Isoflavones: They have a potential thyroid-suppressive effect, which may help regulate excessive thyroid hormone production and alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms.
Gallic acid: It has a potential inhibitory effect on the enzyme 5'-deiodinase, which is involved in the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), a key process in hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their anti-thyroidal and hypolipidemic properties.
Roots: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their ability to balance thyroid function and reduce thyroid hormone production.
Seeds: They are used to make tinctures for hyperthyroidism because of their potential to inhibit the release of thyroid hormones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cassia auriculata leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them completely under shade for 2 weeks.
Chop 100 grams of dried cassia auriculata leaves and flowers into small pieces and store them in airtight containers.
Mix 500ml of 80% ethanol with 100ml of distilled water in a clean glass jar and add the chopped herbs.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place and shake the jar daily for at least 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it is believed to support liver function and promote the removal of excess thyroxine from the body. The tannins and flavonoids present in the plant help to modulate thyroid hormone production and reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland. Additionally, silymarin, a key compound found in Silybum marianum, has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone. This may help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: It helps with hyperthyroidism by reducing the absorption of thyroid hormones from the gut, which can decrease the overall thyroid hormone levels in the body.
Flavonoids (including Isorhapontigenin and Silychristin): They help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Triterpenes (including Ursolic acid): They help by reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormones, which can decrease thyroid activity and alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps to protect the liver and reduce thyroid hormone production.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a mixture of flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help to regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Flowers: They are used due to their high flavonoid content, which can help to protect the liver and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in late summer when they are in full bloom and dry them completely.
Combine 100g of dried silybum marianum plant material with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the silybum marianum tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before using it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain quinine, a natural compound that helps to regulate thyroid hormone levels. The quinine in Cinchona officinalis tinctures has a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing its excessive production of thyroid hormones. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness. By balancing thyroid hormone production, Cinchona officinalis tinctures can help to restore a state of equilibrium in the body and alleviate hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds help with hyperthyroidism by reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland.
Quinine: This alkaloid helps by reducing thyroid hormone production, regulating heart rate, and acting as a mild anticoagulant, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Quinovin: This compound helps by inhibiting the release of thyroid hormones, which can help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in quinine, a natural compound that helps reduce thyroid hormone levels.
Barks: Contain alkaloids that aid in regulating thyroid function and reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Stems: May contain smaller amounts of quinine and other alkaloids that contribute to the overall therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated ensuring freshness and potency.
Chop 250g of cinchona officinalis material into small pieces and combine with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks allowing the flavors to mature and blend.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains compounds that may help regulate thyroid function. The tincture's adaptogenic properties may aid in reducing stress-induced thyroid hormone overproduction, a common cause of hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Panax ginseng's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism. Some studies suggest that Panax ginseng may also help normalize thyroid-stimulating hormone levels, promoting a balance in thyroid hormone production and alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Help reduce thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels and inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Saponins: Exert anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help mitigate the oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism and promote overall thyroid health.
Terpenoids: Display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help counteract the free radical damage and inflammation linked to hyperthyroidism and promote the body's natural balance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The main and most commonly used part of Panax ginseng, which contains bioactive compounds like ginsenosides that help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Roots: The root's secondary part (specifically the lower part) is also used: Lower roots: It's believed to have a higher concentration of ginsenosides than the main root.

Barks: Barks: Panax ginseng barks, although less commonly used, are sometimes used due to their lower concentration of ginsenosides, which might be beneficial for people who experience adverse effects from the more potent root parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4 to 6 pounds of fresh panax ginseng roots with a 10 percent moisture content.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 100 to 110 degrees fahrenheit for 48 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a 1 to 5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rehmannia glutinosa tinctures helps with hyperthyroidism because it's rich in compounds that support thyroid regulation and balance. The tincture's high content of polysaccharides and oligosaccharides can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which are common factors contributing to hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Rehmannia glutinosa contains isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that may help regulate thyroid hormone production and modulate the immune system's response to thyroid issues, promoting a healthier thyroid function.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help reduce thyroid activity by inhibiting the synthesis of thyroid hormones, which can help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Alkaloids (such as rehmannin): Have a sedative effect on the thyroid gland, reducing its activity and subsequent hormone production, which can help alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
Triterpenes (such as ursolic acid): Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their adaptogenic and antithyroid properties, which help to regulate thyroid function.
Rhyzomes: They also contain bioactive compounds that contribute to the antithyroid effects of the tincture.
Seeds: The seeds of Rehmannia glutinosa are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat hyperthyroidism due to their ability to reduce thyroid activity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 pounds of fresh or dried roots of rehmannia glutinosa from a reputable supplier or local cultivation site.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient extraction.
Mix 1 part chopped rehmannia glutinosa roots with 2 parts 95% ethanol in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every few days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
